summary: The reproduction bundle is prepared to demonstrate that attacker-controlled class tokens in @xmldom/xmldom 0.8.12 crash Document.getElementsByClassName with an uncaught TypeError.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should clone the vulnerable 0.8.12 source, invoke getElementsByClassName('secondary') on a document containing class="hasOwnProperty secondary", and observe a non-zero Node.js exit with TypeError: current.hasOwnProperty is not a function.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned https://github.com/xmldom/xmldom.git at commit 189cb78a83e81e1515880988a399e863a8be85ac, built the Docker image, executed the helper against a mounted vulnerable checkout, and observed a non-zero Node.js exit with TypeError: current.hasOwnProperty is not a function from Document.getElementsByClassName after parsing <item class="hasOwnProperty secondary"/>.
verification_method: The container runs the exploit helper, expects a non-zero Node.js exit, and verifies the captured output contains the exact TypeError text plus the getElementsByClassName stack frame.
evidence_produced_by_run: The helper printed [evohunt] parsing crafted XML document and [evohunt] invoking fixed selector: secondary before the failure.
Node.js exited non-zero with TypeError: current.hasOwnProperty is not a function.
The stack trace included /target-repo/lib/dom.js and Document.getElementsByClassName.
verify.sh printed [evohunt] verification passed: observed uncaught TypeError in getElementsByClassName.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
